prompt:
I run the counter software for our print shop and something has gone properly sideways since the front-counter machine got rebuilt last month. Same build of the app, same rate sheet file (it diffs clean against the backup), but the quotes it prints are absurd — a 250-sheet gloss A3 run that has been ~120 for two years now comes out as twelve THOUSAND, and the totals print with a comma where the decimal point should be. Here's the slip it produced next to what it should say:

    flyers [gloss-a3 x250] = 12000,00        flyers [gloss-a3 x250] = 120.00
    menus [matte-a4 x120] = 3110,00          menus [matte-a4 x120] = 31.10
    tags [card-a3 x40] = 6000,00             tags [card-a3 x40] = 60.00
    total = 21110,00                         total = 211.10

On my own dev laptop everything looks fine, which made me think the spreadsheet export was mangling the rate sheet on that one machine — but like I said, the file is byte-identical. QA finally got a rig configured the same way as the counter machine (same OS regional setup) and the test suite reproduces it there every single run: `dotnet test` goes red with exactly these wrong numbers. The suite pins the correct behavior; the arithmetic tests in it still pass, which I don't know what to make of.

Please find what's actually wrong and fix the app so the whole suite is green on that rig (and stays green anywhere else). Don't touch PressQuoteTests.cs, the csproj, or .gitignore — the expected numbers in there are straight off our old printed slips and they are correct.
